title: B4H PDX - Bike Adoption
description: Sliding-scale refurbished bikes and bike parts available at a Portland nonprofit bike shop. Adult bikes from $220–$550 depending on model and condition; children's bikes from $40–$220. Appointment required — test rides are encouraged before committing.
engage: Email info@b4hpdx.org or call 503-496-6941 to schedule an appointment. The shop is at 3366 SE Powell Blvd, Portland.
content:
Services

  • Refurbished Adult Bikes: Sliding-scale pricing from $220–$550. Sizes XS through XL, including mountain, road, and BMX styles.
  • Children's Bikes: $40–$220 depending on size and condition.
  • Parts & Accessories: Deals on parts and accessories available alongside bike adoptions.

How It Works

Bikes for Humanity PDX describes adoption as "a bicycle's return to use, whether as a sale or a grant." Pricing reflects the volunteer labor invested in refurbishing each bike. Test rides are encouraged — contact them to schedule a time before purchasing.

Who It's For

Anyone seeking affordable, reliable transportation. Sliding-scale pricing makes bikes accessible regardless of income.

How to Adopt

Contact Bikes for Humanity PDX by email or phone to schedule an appointment. Current inventory is available at b4hpdx.org/adopt-a-bike.

title: B4H PDX - Volunteer & Bike Repair Workshops
description: Hands-on volunteering and bike repair workshops at Bikes for Humanity PDX. Volunteers help refurbish bikes, assist at the shop, and support community events — no experience required. Periodic workshops teach bicycle mechanics, troubleshooting, and maintenance skills.
engage: Email info@b4hpdx.org or call 503-496-6941 to get involved. No experience or advance registration required for general volunteering.
content:
Volunteer Opportunities

  • Bike Refurbishment: Work hands-on repairing and restoring donated bikes.
  • Shop Assistance: Help with organization, customer support, and day-to-day operations.
  • Community Event Staffing: Support fundraisers, outreach events, and community rides.
  • Creative Projects: Art and creative builds using reclaimed bike parts.
  • Grant Writing & Fundraising: Administrative and development support.

Workshops

Bikes for Humanity PDX periodically offers hands-on bicycle repair and maintenance workshops led by experienced mechanics. Workshops cover bike systems, common repairs, and troubleshooting — open to community members of all skill levels.

Who Can Participate

Open to all community members. No prior experience or commitment required. Contact the shop to ask about current workshop schedules and volunteer openings.
